Planning Guides

Early Access

The Complete Renovation Guide

Planning, budgeting, and executing kitchen, bathroom, and whole-home renovations that increase value and improve daily living.

Table of Contents

  • Planning Your Renovation
  • Budgeting and Financing
  • Design and Material Selection
  • Contractor Management
  • Renovation Timeline
  • Return on Investment

Planning Your Renovation

Successful renovations start with clear planning. Define your goals, assess current condition, and establish realistic timelines and budgets.

Key Planning Steps:

  1. Define your vision: What's the primary goal? Kitchen function? Bathroom modernization? Home value increase?
  2. Assess current condition: Identify underlying issues (plumbing, electrical, structural) that may need addressing.
  3. Determine scope: Single room or whole-home? Cosmetic updates or structural work?
  4. Research local building codes: Permit requirements vary by municipality and project type.
  5. Set realistic timeline: Most renovations take longer than initially expected.
  6. Plan for contingencies: Budget 10-20% extra for unexpected issues.
Pro Tip: Create a mood board with inspiration images. This helps contractors understand your vision and improves communication throughout the project.

Budgeting and Financing

Renovation costs vary widely. Understanding price ranges helps you allocate your budget effectively across different areas.

Typical Renovation Costs (per square foot)

Typical renovation cost ranges by project type
Project Type Cost Range Notes
Kitchen Remodel $150-500/sq ft Varies by appliance and material quality
Bathroom Renovation $100-300/sq ft Tile, fixtures, and plumbing upgrades
General Renovation $75-200/sq ft Flooring, paint, and fixtures
Structural Upgrades Variable HVAC, electrical, and plumbing

Budget Allocation Strategy

Cabinetry and built-ins 30-40%
Appliances and fixtures 20-30%
Labor and installation 30-40%
Materials and finishes 15-25%

Financing Options

  • • Cash: No interest, simplest option if funds are available
  • • Home Equity Loan: Fixed interest rate with predictable payments
  • • HELOC: Flexible access to funds with a variable interest rate
  • • FHA 203(k) Loan: Government-backed financing for major renovations

Design and Material Selection

Choosing the right materials balances aesthetics, durability, maintenance, and cost.

Material Selection Considerations

Countertops

  • Granite/Quartz: Durable with a premium appearance
  • Laminate: Budget-friendly and easy to replace
  • Butcher Block: Warm look with regular maintenance needs

Flooring

  • Tile: Durable and ideal for wet areas
  • Hardwood: Timeless and warm
  • Vinyl: Waterproof with simple maintenance

Cabinetry

  • Custom: Tailored to your space
  • Semi-Custom: Offers options with some customization
  • Stock: Off-the-shelf and budget-friendly

Fixtures

  • Faucets: Both form and function matter
  • Hardware: Cabinet pulls shape the overall style
  • Lighting: Affects both look and daily functionality

Contractor Management

Working effectively with contractors ensures quality work and successful project completion.

Contractor Selection Checklist

Contractor checklist items

  • Licensed, bonded, and insured
  • References from recent projects, and contact them
  • Written estimate with itemized costs
  • Clear communication about timeline and expectations
  • Warranty on workmanship and materials
  • Plan for permits and inspections
  • Liability insurance, with coverage limits verified

Managing the Project

  • • Weekly check-ins: Confirm progress and address issues early
  • • Document everything: Keep photos, emails, and change orders for your records
  • • Payment schedule: Do not pay in full until work is complete and permits have passed
  • • Change order process: Agree on scope changes in writing before proceeding

Typical Renovation Timeline

Planning and Design (2-4 weeks)

Finalize design, materials, budget, and contractor selection.

Permits and Approvals (1-3 weeks)

Submit for permits and wait for approval from local authorities.

Demolition and Prep (1-2 weeks)

Remove old materials, prep the space, and address hidden issues.

Rough Work (2-4 weeks)

Electrical, plumbing, HVAC, framing, and insulation.

Inspections (1-2 days)

Municipal inspections of electrical, plumbing, and structural work.

Finish Work (3-6 weeks)

Drywall, painting, flooring, cabinetry, fixtures, and final details.

Final Inspection and Walkthrough (1 week)

Final municipal approval and a detailed walkthrough with the contractor.

Total typical timeline: 10-20 weeks for a comprehensive renovation. Unexpected issues can extend timelines.

Return on Investment

Different renovations provide different returns. Understanding ROI helps prioritize your projects.
Typical return on investment by renovation type
Renovation Type Average ROI Notes
Kitchen Remodel 60-80% High buyer interest with significant impact
Bathroom Remodel 50-70% Modern fixtures consistently appeal to buyers
HVAC/Mechanical 50-75% Functional upgrades improve marketability
Flooring 40-60% Visible impact that varies by material choice
